title_en: 5. Would you like to meet him outside the institution? (cont.)
title_id: 5. Maukah Kamu Menemuinya di Luar Lembaga? (lanjutan)
content_en: He said to them, "The example of me with Respond to mistreatment with kindness this Bedouin is like that of a man whose camel runs away from him. A group of people then chase after the camel, as it runs away from them being frightened. They only manage to make it ﬂee further. So the camel's owner ﬁnally says,'Leave me alone with the camel! I am gentler with it and I know how it thinks!' The camel's owner then grabs some fallen dates from the ground and calls it until it ﬁnally comes to him. He then ties the saddle around it and mounts. If I were to have obeyed you when he said what he said, he would have entered the ﬁre of Hell." Meaning, if you were to have made him ﬂee, he might have left the religion and entered the ﬁre of Hell. (Narrated by al-Bazzar with a disputed chain) Gentleness only beautiﬁes an action while harshness only serves to tarnish it. "The good deed and the evil deed are not alike. Repel the evil deed with one which is better, then lo! He between whom and you there was enmity (will become) as though he was a bosom friend." It is mentioned that when the Prophet conquered Makkah, and began to circumambulate the Ka'bah, there came Fadhalah bin 'Umayr – a person who pretended to be a Muslim – and began to make Tawaaf behind the Prophet waiting for an opportunity to kill him by surprise. When he came close to the Prophet , he became aware of him. He turned around and said to him, "Are you Fadhalah?" He replied, "Yes, I am Fadhalah, O Messenger of Allah!" The Prophet asked, "What were you thinking of doing?" He replied, "Nothing! I was only remembering Allah!" The Prophet then laughed and said, "I seek Allah's forgive36 Respond to mistreatment with kindness ness." Fadhalah later said about this incident: "The Messenger of Allah then placed his hand over my chest, and my heart came to rest. By Allah, the Messenger of Allah did not lift his hand from my chest except that Allah had placed in my heart the strongest love for him." Fadhalah headed to his family and passed by a woman he would sit and chat with. When she saw him, she said, "Why don't you come over for a chat?" He replied, "No." She insisted, "Come over for a chat." "No," Fadhala said, "Allah and Islam forbid you from it. 'If you were to see Muhammad and his party victorious the day the idols were smashed 'You would have witnessed Allah's religion become manifest As polytheism became engulfed with darkness.' Thereafter, Fadhalah became one of the righteous Muslims. The Prophet used to capture people's hearts by forgiving them. He would bear their harms in order to change them and bring them closer to what was good for them. Abu Talib would frequently protect the Prophet from the harms of the Quraysh. After Abu Talib passed away, the Quraysh increased its pressure on the Prophet . He was harmed the way he was never harmed whilst his uncle was alive. The Prophet began to consider another source of refuge, aid and support. He left for Ta'if in order to seek the support and Respond to mistreatment with kindness assistance of the tribe of Thaqif. He entered Ta'if and proceeded to meet three men who were the leaders and the noblest men of Thaqif. They were three brothers; 'Abd Yalayl bin 'Amr, and his brothers Mas'ud and Habib. The Prophet sat with them and called them to Allah. He asked them to help him in championing the cause of Islam and to stand by him against those who oppose him from his own people. However, their response was disgraceful! One of them said, "I would tear down the covering of Ka'bah if Allah has really sent you!" The other mocked, "Didn't Allah ﬁnd anyone else to send?" The third person thought about what his response should be. He wanted his words to be more malicious than that of his brothers. He said, "By Allah, I will never respond to you! If you really are the Messenger of Allah as you claim, then you are too dangerous to be responded to! If you are lying upon Allah, then I should never speak to you!" The Prophet then stood up to leave after feeling disappointed by the best people from Thaqif. He also feared that the Quraysh may ﬁnd out that Thaqif had rejected him, and subsequently begin to harm him more. Thus he said to them, "Whatever you do, please keep this meeting a secret." But they did not. Instead, they incited the slaves and the foolish against him, until they began to follow him to hurl curses and abuse at him. Respond to mistreatment with kindness They gathered along his path in two lines and the Prophet was walking very quickly in between to save himself.
content_id: Beliau bersabda kepada mereka, "Perumpamaanku dengan orang Badui ini seperti seorang lelaki yang untanya kabur. Sekelompok orang kemudian mengejarnya, tetapi unta itu semakin lari karena ketakutan. Mereka hanya berhasil membuatnya semakin jauh. Akhirnya sang pemilik unta berkata, 'Biarkan aku sendiri yang mengurus unta ini! Aku lebih lemah lembut dengannya dan aku tahu cara berpikirnya.' Pemilik unta itu kemudian memungut beberapa kurma yang jatuh di tanah dan memanggilnya hingga unta itu akhirnya datang. Dia lalu mengikat pelana di punggungnya dan menaikinya. Seandainya aku mengikuti saran kalian ketika dia berkata seperti itu, niscaya dia akan masuk neraka." Maksudnya: seandainya kalian membuatnya kabur, dia mungkin akan meninggalkan agama dan masuk neraka. (Diriwayatkan oleh al-Bazzar dengan sanad yang diperselisihkan.) Kelembutan hanya memperindah suatu tindakan, sedangkan kekerasan hanya memperburuknya. "Tidaklah sama kebaikan dan kejahatan. Tolaklah kejahatan dengan kebaikan, maka orang yang antara kamu dan dia ada permusuhan seolah-olah akan menjadi teman yang akrab." Dikisahkan bahwa ketika Rasulullah menaklukkan Makkah dan mulai melakukan tawaf di Ka'bah, datanglah Fadhalah bin Umair — seseorang yang berpura-pura menjadi Muslim — dan mulai bertawaf di belakang Rasulullah sambil menunggu kesempatan untuk membunuhnya secara tiba-tiba. Ketika dia mendekat, Rasulullah menyadarinya. Beliau berbalik dan berkata, "Apakah kamu Fadhalah?" Dia menjawab, "Ya, saya Fadhalah, wahai Rasulullah!" Rasulullah bertanya, "Apa yang sedang kamu pikirkan?" Dia menjawab, "Tidak ada apa-apa! Aku hanya sedang berzikir kepada Allah!" Rasulullah pun tertawa dan berkata, "Aku memohon ampun kepada Allah." Fadhalah kemudian menceritakan kejadian itu: "Rasulullah kemudian meletakkan tangannya di atas dadaku, dan hatiku pun menjadi tenang. Demi Allah, Rasulullah tidak mengangkat tangannya dari dadaku kecuali Allah telah menanamkan dalam hatiku rasa cinta yang paling kuat kepadanya." Fadhalah menuju keluarganya dan melewati seorang wanita yang biasa dia duduk dan mengobrol dengannya. Ketika wanita itu melihatnya, dia berkata, "Mengapa tidak kamu datang untuk mengobrol?" Dia menjawab, "Tidak." Wanita itu mendesak, "Datanglah untuk mengobrol." "Tidak," kata Fadhalah, "Allah dan Islam melarangnya. 'Seandainya kamu menyaksikan Muhammad dan pasukannya menang di hari berhala-berhala dihancurkan, kamu pasti menyaksikan agama Allah menjadi nyata ketika kemusyrikan diliputi kegelapan.'" Setelah itu, Fadhalah menjadi salah satu Muslim yang shalih. Rasulullah biasa menaklukkan hati manusia dengan memaafkan mereka. Beliau menanggung gangguan mereka demi mengubah mereka dan mendekatkan mereka kepada kebaikan. Abu Thalib sering melindungi Rasulullah dari gangguan Quraisy. Setelah Abu Thalib wafat, Quraisy semakin meningkatkan tekanan kepada Rasulullah. Beliau disakiti dengan cara yang tidak pernah terjadi selama pamannya masih hidup. Rasulullah mulai mencari sumber perlindungan, bantuan, dan dukungan lain. Beliau berangkat ke Thaif untuk mencari dukungan dari suku Tsaqif. Beliau memasuki Thaif dan menemui tiga orang yang merupakan pemimpin dan orang-orang terhormat Tsaqif. Mereka adalah tiga bersaudara: Abd Yalayl bin Amr beserta saudara-saudaranya Mas'ud dan Habib. Rasulullah duduk bersama mereka dan menyeru mereka kepada Allah. Beliau meminta mereka untuk membantu memperjuangkan Islam dan berdiri bersamanya melawan orang-orang yang menentangnya dari kaumnya sendiri. Namun tanggapan mereka sangat memalukan! Salah satu dari mereka berkata, "Aku akan merobek kain Ka'bah jika Allah benar-benar mengutusmu!" Yang lain mengejek, "Tidak adakah orang lain yang Allah temukan untuk diutus?" Orang ketiga memikirkan apa yang harus dia katakan. Dia ingin kata-katanya lebih jahat dari saudara-saudaranya. Dia berkata, "Demi Allah, aku tidak akan pernah membalas perkataanmu! Jika kamu benar-benar Rasulullah sebagaimana klaimmu, maka kamu terlalu berbahaya untuk diajak bicara! Jika kamu berdusta atas nama Allah, maka aku tidak seharusnya berbicara denganmu!" Rasulullah pun berdiri untuk pergi setelah merasa kecewa dengan orang-orang terbaik dari Tsaqif. Beliau juga khawatir Quraisy akan mengetahui bahwa Tsaqif telah menolaknya, yang akan membuat mereka semakin menyakitinya. Maka beliau berkata kepada mereka, "Apapun yang terjadi, tolong rahasiakan pertemuan ini." Namun mereka tidak melakukannya. Mereka malah menghasut para budak dan orang-orang bodoh untuk mengejarnya, hingga mereka mulai mengikutinya sambil melontarkan makian dan hinaan. Mereka berkumpul di sepanjang jalannya dalam dua barisan dan Rasulullah berjalan dengan sangat cepat di antaranya demi menyelamatkan diri.
